author: potuz
content: This is a bit dangerous I believe. In a situation in which 50% of the chain is missing, proposer boost is already almost a full committee. If you cause any split then you can reorg either side with proposer boost on the next slot. So the builder of N colludes with the proposer of N+2 to both get N full canonical and also steal the block at N+1.

author: potuz
content: This is one of the reasons we make all attesters follow the proposer.

author: terencechain
content:
Gloas dropped the `future-slot [IGNORE]` check on data column sidecars that fulu had. The list now opens with "a valid block for the sidecar's slot has been seen" 

Problem: if a sidecar arrives before its block, it gets queued pending the block. We prune that queue by slot age, but with no future-slot bound a sidecar can claim an arbitrarily far-future slot and never get pruned 

Should  we add the future-slot [IGNORE] back as the first check, before the block-seen check? or this is client implementation leaking into the spec?

author: jtraglia
content: Hmm interesting. I think that's a good reason to add it back.

author: jtraglia
content: But it is sort of a client implementation leak.

author: jtraglia
content: Curious how other clients handle this. But I'd lean towards adding it back.

author: jtraglia
content: For reference, this is the (executable) spec:

author: 0xunclebill
content: ou reprocess queue will remove sidecars after a certain time in the queue, regardless of what slot a sidecar claims. but happy to add it back either way

author: terencechain
content: yes, this depends on the shape of the cache, if the cache depends on `column.slot` then its not good
